title: linaria cymbalaria
intro: Linaria cymbalaria, also known as Toadflax or Jockey's Buttons, is a flowering plant in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Linaria cymbalaria has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties, which help to relieve congestion and promote healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for use in herbal remedies, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
